signal_emails.subject: IAIC 2026 - Reviewer Comments for Paper #347
signal_emails.body:
Dear Dr. Chen,

Please find below the reviewer comments for your submitted paper:

Paper #347: "Scaling Laws for Long-Term Memory in AI Agents"
Authors: David Chen (AI Horizons Lab), Maria Garcia (MIT), Yuki Tanaka (University of Tokyo)

═══════════════════════════════════════
REVIEWER 1 — Decision: ACCEPT
═══════════════════════════════════════
Overall Assessment: Strong contribution to the field of long-term memory in AI agents. The scaling law formulation is novel and well-supported by experiments.

Required Revisions:
1. The related work section needs strengthening with recent 2025 publications, particularly the work on retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) for agent memory by Zhang et al. (NeurIPS 2025) and the episodic memory framework by Park et al. (ICML 2025). At least 5-8 new references should be added.

2. Table 3 has LaTeX formatting issues — the column alignment is broken in the PDF rendering. Please fix the tabular environment and ensure all numerical values are properly aligned.

3. The paper would benefit from a dedicated limitations section discussing potential failure modes of the proposed scaling laws, especially for out-of-distribution scenarios and adversarial inputs.

Minor: Typo on page 7, line 23: "achive" → "achieve"

═══════════════════════════════════════
REVIEWER 2 — Decision: MAJOR REVISION
═══════════════════════════════════════
Overall Assessment: Interesting work but insufficient baselines and missing ablation study.

Required Revisions:
4. The paper lacks comparison with MemoryBank (Wang et al., ACL 2025), which is the current state-of-the-art for long-term agent memory. This baseline is essential for positioning the contribution. Please add full experimental comparison on all 3 benchmark datasets.

5. An ablation study is needed to validate the contribution of each component (context compression, memory retrieval, decay function). Without this, it's unclear which part of the system drives the performance gains.

6. Figure 5 has axis labels that are too small to read in print. Please increase font size to at least 10pt and add grid lines for readability.

═══════════════════════════════════════
REVIEWER 3 — Decision: ACCEPT
═══════════════════════════════════════
Overall Assessment: Well-written paper with solid experimental methodology.

Required Revisions:
7. Section 4.2 needs restructuring for better flow. The transition from the theoretical framework to empirical validation is abrupt. Consider adding a bridging paragraph that motivates the experimental setup based on the theoretical predictions.

═══════════════════════════════════════

Camera-Ready Deadline: February 27, 2026, 11:59 PM PST
Submission Portal: EasyChair Paper #347

Please address all required revisions in your camera-ready submission.

Best regards,
IAIC 2026 Program Committee

signal_emails.subject: Re: Camera-ready revisions progress
signal_emails.body:
Hi David,

Here is my progress update on the reviewer comments. I've been working on these since we received the reviews last Monday.

✅ COMPLETED:

1. Updated Related Work Section (Reviewer 1, Comment 1)
   - Added 8 new 2025 references including Zhang et al. (NeurIPS 2025) and Park et al. (ICML 2025)
   - Restructured the section to better position our contribution
   - Word count increased from 450 to 720 words
   - Committed to branch: camera-ready-v2

2. Fixed Table 3 LaTeX Formatting (Reviewer 1, Comment 2)
   - Replaced tabular with booktabs package
   - Fixed column alignment with siunitx for numerical values
   - Verified PDF rendering looks correct

3. Added Limitations Section (Reviewer 1, Comment 3)
   - 350-word section covering: distribution shift, adversarial robustness, computational overhead scaling
   - Placed before Conclusion as Section 6
   - Also fixed the typo on page 7

🔄 IN PROGRESS:

4. Ablation Study (Reviewer 2, Comment 5)
   - Running experiments on the 3 component variants
   - GPU cluster allocation: 4x A100 for 48 hours
   - ETA: Tuesday evening, February 25
   - Will produce Table 5 (new) with ablation results

⏳ BLOCKED / NOT STARTED:

5. MemoryBank Baseline Comparison (Reviewer 2, Comment 4)
   - Issue: MemoryBank code is not publicly available
   - I emailed the authors (wang.memory@berkeley.example.edu) on Feb 18 requesting access
   - No response yet — this is the highest risk item
   - If we don't get access by Feb 24, we may need to implement from the paper description (estimated: 3-4 days, won't make deadline)
   - Alternative: cite as "concurrent work" and note unavailability of code

🔧 NEEDS DAVID:

6. Figure 5 Axis Labels (Reviewer 2, Comment 6)
   - David, can you update the matplotlib script? You have the original plotting code
   - Changes needed: font size 8pt→12pt, add grid lines, increase DPI to 300
   - I don't have access to the raw data files for regenerating the figure

7. Section 4.2 Restructuring (Reviewer 3, Comment 7)
   - David, this is your section — the theoretical framework part
   - Need: bridging paragraph between theory (4.2.1) and experiments (4.2.2)
   - Suggested approach: add 2-3 sentences connecting scaling law predictions to experimental hypotheses

Overall Status: 3/7 items complete, 1 in progress, 1 blocked, 2 waiting on David.

Can we sync on Monday (Feb 24) during our co-author meeting to review everything?

Best,
Maria Garcia
Postdoctoral Researcher, MIT CSAIL

signal_emails.subject: IAIC 2026 Keynote Speaker - Logistics Information
signal_emails.body:
Dear Dr. Chen,

Thank you for accepting our keynote invitation for IAIC 2026. Here are the logistics details for your presentation:

1. SLIDE DECK SUBMISSION
   - Deadline: March 2, 2026
   - Send to: speakers@iaic2026.example.org
   - Format: PDF + original PPTX/Keynote
   - Template: Attached (IAIC2026_speaker_template.pptx)

2. SPEAKER REGISTRATION
   - Your registration code: SPKR-CHEN-2026
   - Register at: https://iaic2026.example.org/register
   - Registration includes: all sessions, workshops, conference dinner, proceedings

3. KEYNOTE SCHEDULE
   - Date: March 5, 2026
   - Time: 9:00 AM - 10:00 AM (45 min talk + 15 min Q&A)
   - Venue: Convention Center San Francisco, Hall A
   - Session Chair: Prof. Sarah Mitchell (Stanford)

4. A/V EQUIPMENT
   - Hall A supports: HDMI and USB-C connections
   - Projector: 4K resolution, 16:9 aspect ratio
   - Please arrive for A/V check on March 4, 3:00-4:00 PM
   - Tech support contact: tech@conventioncentersf.example.com

5. DEMO REQUIREMENTS (if applicable)
   - If your keynote includes a live demo, please notify us by February 28
   - We can provide: dedicated WiFi (100 Mbps), extra display, USB hub
   - Contact: av-support@iaic2026.example.org

6. TRAVEL & ACCOMMODATION
   - Conference hotel: Grand Hyatt San Francisco (special rate available)
   - Conference dinner: March 4, 7:00-9:00 PM, Waterbar Restaurant
   - Dress code: Business casual

Please don't hesitate to reach out if you have any questions.

Best regards,
IAIC 2026 Organizing Committee
speakers@iaic2026.example.org

event_summary_ground_truth: IAIC 2026会议全流程：论文#347《Scaling Laws for Long-Term Memory in AI Agents》收到3位审稿人7条修改意见（R1接受：相关工作+Table3+局限性；R2大修：MemoryBank基线+消融实验+Figure5；R3接受：Section4.2）。Maria已完成3项（相关工作8篇新引用、Table3 LaTeX、局限性章节），消融实验进行中ETA 2/25，MemoryBank基线阻塞（等代码库无ETA——最大风险）。David负责Figure5 matplotlib脚本和Section4.2修改。关键时间线：Co-author Sync 2/24 → Camera-Ready截止2/27 11:59PM PST → Demo需求通知截止2/28 → 幻灯片提交3/2（发给speakers@iaic2026.example.org）→ 旅行3/4（UA-1234到SFO 11:30，酒店Grand Hyatt GH-789012，A/V检查15:00 Hall A支持HDMI+USB-C，Conference Dinner 19:00 Waterbar）→ 主旨演讲3/5 09:00-10:00（注册码SPKR-CHEN-2026）。
